other_observers: Marcel Such. Joel Such.; Yes, along with Marcel Such and Joel Such.
specific_location: One of the co-observers, Marcel Such, has determined that we initially saw it at 38deg 41' 51.75" North, 104deg 42' 51.50" West. This is in the floodplain of the east side of Fountain Creek
description: The bird was a "Red" Fox Sparrow of the nominate -iliaca- group. It was notably larger than nearby juncos, and it had large, "blocky" red streaks and splotches on the breast and flanks. Its face was hard to make out in the early light (see below), but it was not as uniformly red as the red coloration on the underparts. The tail was red, notably so in flight. On the perched bird, the wings were reddish. Flying away, the birds red tail contrasted well its less-colorful upperparts.Behaviors: We heard the bird first, and I was fairly certain it was a Fox Sparrow, based on the call-note. When we first saw it, it was in a nearby shrub, seen in profile view. It was calling repeatedly. We spooked the bird, and it flew away to our right, a distance of about 100 feet away. We did not hear it give a flight call, and I do not recall if it gave a call-note after landing.Call: Inititially and provisionally identified by call, a repeated "tsook" note, louder and richer than the "tik" notes of nearby Dark-eyed Juncos.Plumage: unknown, but something postjuvenal
